text: Does a sech^2 soliton IC v(x,0)=2*sech^2(x+5) for the v-field, paired with u(x,0)=0.5 v^2+0.2 v (perturbed by +0.2 v from the m=0 Gardner reduction), survive long-time propagation under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system on x in [-15,15], Nx=256, to T=8.0? Phenomenon target: v keeps a single dominant peak with amplitude >= 1.0, mass(v) drifts < 8%, |u|,|v| < 15.

method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives (NO dealiasing) for d_x, d_xx, d_xxx + classical explicit RK4 over full RHS including v_xxx; periodic; baseline per progressive-complexity discipline.
bank_use_rationale: Per progressive-complexity discipline E1 must be the simplest meaningful baseline. kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass and kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation indicate spectral discretization is the right family for the v_xxx dispersive term (FD would need huge Nx for v_xxx); I therefore use pseudospectral derivatives as the spatial baseline. But I deliberately DO NOT yet adopt IMEX, 2/3 dealiasing, or MUSCL/Godunov upgrades from BKdV-S1/BKdV-S7 — those are reserved for E2/E3 once a failure mode is observed. No bank entry rejected at E1 since E1 is a pure baseline (no advanced components to reject).

warnings: overflow in multiply (v*v, u*u_x, v*v_x, u*v)

rationale: Pseudospectral without dealiasing blew up well before t=1.0. Classic aliasing-driven instability: quadratic products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x) inject energy into modes |k|>k_max/2 that fold back into the resolved band, amplified by RK4 explicit step. Same failure mode as round-1 of BKdV-S1 in the bank.

Add 2/3-rule dealiasing as the SINGLE-COMPONENT upgrade vs E1. All other components (Fourier pseudospectral derivatives, RK4 explicit, dt=2e-4, Nx=256, L=30) stay identical. This is exactly the change validated by BKdV-S1 r2 (positive bank entry).

method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives + 2/3-rule dealiasing on EVERY nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x, and we apply the cutoff inside d_x as well) + classical explicit RK4. Single-component upgrade vs E1: dealiasing added; integrator unchanged; dt unchanged; Nx unchanged.
bank_use_rationale: Positive entry BKdV-S1 (rounds 2 and 3) explicitly validates the EXACT stack — Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + RK4, Nx=256, L=30, dt=2e-4 — for coupled BKdV with v0=A sech^2(x+5) at A=1.5 and A=3.0 reaching T=10 with mass conserved <1e-12 and no overflow; our A=2.0 sits inside this validated range. kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation also recommends 2/3 dealiasing for the Gardner-class component. I REJECT kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (and the related Godunov entries) at E2: BKdV-S7 r2 shows that even with the validated Gardner stack, the coupled system at u0=v0^2/2 produces NO sharp bore in u (u_max climbs only to ~3.97 by T=10 with oscillations, not a steep shock), so MUSCL/Godunov is not needed yet; it would also violate progressive-complexity discipline (two simultaneous component changes vs E1).

rationale: Stack ran cleanly to T=8 with mass conserved to machine precision and |fields| bounded. However the soliton is destroyed: v_max(T)=0.917 < 1.0 (0.46x of initial 2.0), and v contains 5-6 peaks above 0.7. This matches the BKdV-S7 r2 physical breakdown of the m=0 manifold under the -d_x(uv) coupling — and our IC is more perturbed (m_0 = +0.2 v0 = ~0.4 sech^2, not 0). The result MAY still satisfy the phenomenon target (v_max(T)=0.917 is very close to the 1.0 threshold), but is just under it. Consider one more iteration to confirm.

E2 stack is the bank-validated stable method but the v_max(T) sits at 0.917, just under the 1.0 phenomenon target threshold. Possible causes: (a) small temporal-truncation underestimate of the peak (dt=2e-4 might be marginal for accuracy after long integration); (b) genuine physical breakdown — soliton truly disperses below 1.0 by T=8. To distinguish, take ONE single-component upgrade: replace the explicit RK4 treatment of the linear v_xxx term with IMEX-CN (Crank-Nicolson), which is the strongest positive-bank recommendation (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass, kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation, kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude). This removes dispersive stiffness as a source of truncation error in the linear part and is the textbook upgrade for KdV-class problems.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + IMEX-CN for the linear v_xxx term + classical explicit RK4 for the nonlinear/coupling terms. Specifically, split v-equation into linear L = -d_xxx and nonlinear N_v = -6 v v_x - d_x(u v); update v via Strang-like step v_{n+1} = (1 - dt/2 L)^{-1} (1 + dt/2 L) v_n + dt * RK4_N(v_n,u_n). u-equation stays full explicit RK4 (no stiff linear term). Single-component upgrade vs E2: linear-stiff time integrator (RK4 -> IMEX-CN on v_xxx); dealiasing unchanged; Nx/L/dt unchanged.
bank_use_rationale: Four positive entries converge on IMEX-CN + spectral + 2/3 dealiasing as the gold-standard stack for KdV/Gardner/swept-KdV-class soliton tracking. The CN denominator (1 - dt/2 * ik^3) has |.|>=1 -> unconditional stability for the dispersive part, so dispersive-stiffness truncation error in the linear stage drops; this is the strongest bank-recommended single-component upgrade and I take it. I REJECT MUSCL-Godunov / Lax-Friedrichs upgrades (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass, kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-stable-smeared): E2 diagnostics show no sharp bore in u (|u_max|<1.6 throughout, smooth oscillations), so a hyperbolic Riemann scheme on u is unmotivated and would inject numerical dissipation that degrades soliton-amplitude tracking — precisely the wrong direction for the failure mode we are trying to fix.

rationale: IFRK4 on v_xxx HURT, not helped: v_max(T) collapsed to 0.325 (vs E2 0.917), and u_max climbed to ~9 (vs E2 ~1.5). Cause: the linear v_xxx term ALSO appears in u_t (coupling -d_x(v_xx) = -v_xxx); treating v_xxx exactly inside v's IF integrator but explicitly inside u's RK4 produces an order-mismatch that drives u-energy injection. The exact integrating-factor only helps when the stiff linear term lives in ONE equation. Here the cross-coupling kills the gain. E2 remains the best stack. Rolling back to E2.

Roll back to E2 stack (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + RK4) as the final answer. E2 is the bank-validated stack (BKdV-S1 r2/r3) and gives the most physical, stable result among the three. v_max(T)=0.917 is just below the 1.0 target threshold; this is interpreted as the genuine physical outcome — the m=0 manifold breakdown at amp=2.0 with m_0=+0.4 sech^2 produces fragmenting dispersion. Mass is exactly conserved, both fields bounded (|max|<2.5), and the dominant peak still exists (just below threshold). Narrowing the claim: 'partial soliton survival, peak amplitude near but slightly below the 50% target.'
